notes: JUDGEMENT 12-card gravity deck. The cemetery on All Souls' Eve working on the reader: the wind, the list growing, the names that aren't on it, the names that are.

cards.title: A READING DRIFTS OVER FROM THE W ROW
cards.flavor: The west row's reader is on the third name now. The reading lands inside your reading like an echo. The ensemble is what the ensemble is.

effects.text: You hear them. They hear you. The wind carries it.

cards.title: THE WIND THROUGH THE CYPRESSES
cards.flavor: Small, gusting. The lamps flicker. The candles on the vaults dip and don't go out.

then.text: You held the name through the gust. The name came out the other side.

cards.title: THE LIST IS LONGER THAN IT WAS
cards.flavor: At the lectern. Beatrice's clipped list. Two pages instead of one. You did not, when you got here, notice the second page.

cards.title: THE CARETAKER WITH THE LANTERN
cards.flavor: Mr Decoteau, the parish caretaker, walking the central spine with the brass lantern. He nods at each reader as he passes.

cards.title: THE WIDOW AT THE MAUSOLEUM
cards.flavor: Mrs Trahan. Her husband is in the central mausoleum's third tier. She has been here every All Souls' Eve since 2017.

cards.title: A LAMP FLICKERS ON THE CENTRAL SPINE
cards.flavor: The third one south. The wick has been long since spring. The flicker is a half-second.

cards.title: YOU RECOGNISE A NAME
cards.flavor: On the list. Two down from where the readers are. Someone you knew. Not someone you expected to see on tonight's reading.

cards.title: A CHILD WITH A ROSARY
cards.flavor: Eight, maybe nine. With her grandmother. Walking the west row slowly. The rosary is in her left hand.

cards.title: THE CENTRAL MAUSOLEUM'S DOOR
cards.flavor: Bronze. Closed since '78. The brass handle is at the height of an adult's hip. The door does not, this evening, open.

effects.text: You touch the door's brass. It is cold. It stays cold.

cards.title: A CANDLE BLEW OUT
cards.flavor: On the W row, the third vault. Not yours. You can re-light it. It is an ensemble; the ensemble re-lights its own.

effects.text: You walk over. You re-light it. You return.

cards.title: YOUR GRANDMOTHER'S NAME IS NOT ON THE LIST
cards.flavor: You scan the second page. She is not there. The omission is not, in Beatrice's hand, accidental. She is leaving it for you to add.

effects.text: The omission is yours to repair.

cards.title: THE CHORD IN THE LAMPS
cards.flavor: All six lamps along the spine flicker in unison. A second. Then they settle.

options.label: Read her name (cost: 1 Time; gain: −3 CALL, increment names_read)

effects.text: You step to the lectern. You read your grandmother's name aloud. The W row's reader pauses for the count of two.
